Wyoming Daily 'Final flight' Oct 1 2016; DOT terminated EAS eligibility Sept 30 2016

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Gillette-Campbell County Airport
Wikipedia Gillette-Campbell County Airport
Gillette News Record Oct 2025 — United taking full market risk 2026

note: Frontier served GCC with 737-200s to DEN/BIL until shutdown 1986; state capacity purchase agreement with SkyWest ended 2025, United assumed full risk Jan 2026

note: Frontier served LAR until 1986 shutdown; EAS-subsidized DEN service only since then via various United Express carriers

lostDestinations.source: Wyoming Daily 'Final flight' Oct 1 2016

detail: DOT terminated WRL EAS eligibility effective September 30, 2016 after per-passenger subsidy exceeded the statutory $1,000 cap; Great Lakes had served the route since 1997 at $2.3M annual subsidy.
source: Wyoming Daily Oct 1 2016; Kathryn's Report April 2016 DOT EAS order

Wikipedia Casper-Natrona County Intl Airport

detail: Delta (SkyWest CRJ-200) ended CPR-SLC December 3, 2024 after Natrona County withdrew $1.3M minimum revenue guarantee; CPR now served only by United Express DEN.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Sheridan County Airport — SkyWest took over Jan 12 2020

lostDestinations.carrier: Western Airlines then Big Sky Airlines
lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Sheridan County Airport — Western dropped SHR 1980; Big Sky Billings-SHR-CPR 1980

detail: Western Airlines (mainline jets) dropped Sheridan in 1980; EAS-subsidized DEN service restored May 2007 under Great Lakes after Big Sky folded Jan 2008; Denver Air Connection ended Jan 2020 replaced by SkyWest United Express.

lostDestinations.source: Southwest Wyoming Regional Airport official site flyrks.com
Wikipedia Southwest Wyoming Regional Airport — Frontier served RKS to SLC/DEN/BIL
Wikipedia Southwest Wyoming Regional Airport

detail: Rock Springs retains twice-daily United Express DEN service; formerly received multiple regional connections including SLC and BIL under Frontier until 1986 collapse; state capacity purchase agreement helped stabilize service.
source: flyrks.com; Wikipedia Southwest Wyoming Regional Airport

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Craig-Moffat Airport — Rocky Mountain Airways DHC-6 Twin Otter service mid-1970s

detail: Rocky Mountain Airways served Craig-DEN with DHC-6 Twin Otters in the mid-1970s; service ended by approximately 1979; airport has been general aviation only since, with ~12,000 annual operations all GA as of 2021.
source: Wikipedia Craig-Moffat Airport; Facebook Wings Museum #ColoradoAirportSeries post

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Perry Stokes Airport — commuter-airline flights 1969-71; possibly none since
Wikipedia Perry Stokes Airport — Continental Denver-Albuquerque DC-3 route via TAD 1949-1957

detail: Continental's Denver-Albuquerque DC-3 route stopped at Trinidad from 1949 to 1957; brief commuter service 1969-71; no scheduled service since approximately 1971 as declining coal-mine economy reduced demand.
source: Wikipedia Perry Stokes Airport; Rocky Mountain PBS rural airports article

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Greeley-Weld County Airport — Trans Central Cessna 402s to DEN 1969-70
Wikipedia Greeley-Weld County Airport — Challenger DC-3 service began Aug 1947

detail: Challenger Airlines launched scheduled DC-3 service August 1947 on Billings-Greeley-Denver route; Trans Central Airlines provided final scheduled service 1969-70 with Cessna 402s to Denver; no commercial service since 1970 despite Greeley being Colorado's 4th-largest city.
source: Wikipedia Greeley-Weld County Airport; flygxy.com Airport History

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Gunnison-Crested Butte Regional Airport — Delta nonstop B757 ATL service mid-1990s
Wikipedia Gunnison-Crested Butte Regional Airport — AA nonstop B757 DFW service 1995
Wikipedia Gunnison-Crested Butte Regional Airport — AA direct PHL one-stop via DFW 1995
Wikipedia Gunnison-Crested Butte Regional Airport

detail: GUC had peak service in 1995 with mainline B757 jets from American (DFW/PHL) and Delta (ATL); both lost as ski-season demand shifted to Eagle (EGE) and Aspen (ASE); retains seasonal United DEN/IAH, American DFW, and JSX DAL service.
source: Wikipedia Gunnison-Crested Butte Regional Airport; gunnisonvalleyrta.com

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Telluride Regional Airport — Mesa served TEX-ABQ/DEN/PHX from Dec 1985
Wikipedia Telluride Regional Airport — Boutique Air seasonal PHX Aug 2018 - Oct 2019

detail: Commercial service began Dec 19 1985 (Mesa Airlines); Boutique Air withdrew Oct 31 2019 citing low load factors; Key Lime Air (Denver Air Connection) now sole carrier with DEN year-round and seasonal PHX.
